patterns: Founder-behavior calls are highly reliable: PG's high-conviction claims about founder psychology and startup definition hit ~0.71, with 5 fully correct out of 7 graded takes.
Startup-tactics calls are mostly right but misfire on competitive-dynamics predictions — 4/5 correct, but the super-angels-vs-VC call inverted completely within a decade.
PG over-extrapolates from static structural conditions: both his VC-threat and tax-risk-appetite claims ignored how ecosystems adapt, producing his clearest misses.
Solo-founder and cofounder claims were correct for 10+ years but have softened post-2018 as AI tooling eroded the scope advantage of multiple founders — a rare time-bounded partial miss.

highlight_takes.claim_text: Because risk and reward must be proportionate, decreasing the maximum possible rewards decreases people's willingness to take risks.
highlight_takes.verdict_summary: Premise didn't survive contact with data — CA raised rates through 2010s and SV hit record startup formation anyway.

highlight_takes.claim_text: So I think VC funds are seriously threatened by the super-angels.
highlight_takes.verdict_summary: The opposite happened — top VCs ended the 2010s stronger than ever; super-angels either became VCs or stayed niche.

highlight_takes.claim_text: Great cities attract ambitious people and send them messages about what's worth doing — and these messages differ meaningfully by city.
highlight_takes.verdict_summary: Prescient on durability — SF/SV retained dominance through 2020 — but missed the post-COVID remote shift and hub diversification.

highlight_takes.claim_text: It is empirically hard to start a startup with just one founder; most big successes have two or three founders.
highlight_takes.verdict_summary: Held through 2018 then softened — AI tooling let solo founders ship scope that previously required cofounders.

highlight_takes.claim_text: Single founders are at a serious disadvantage; starting a startup is too hard for one person, and few successful startups were founded by just one person.
highlight_takes.verdict_summary: Held through 2018 then softened — AI tooling and no-code platforms unlocked solo-founder scope that previously required cofounders.
